content.problemStatement:
After the phenomenal success of the 36th Chamber of Shaolin, San Te has decided to start 37th Chamber of Shaolin. The aim this time is to equip women with shaolin self-defence techniques.

The only condition for a woman to be eligible for the special training is that she must be between 101010 and 606060 years of age, inclusive of both 101010 and 606060.

Given the ages of NNN women in his village, please help San Te find out how many of them are eligible for the special training.


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN, the number of women.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2,..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​, the ages of the women.


Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the number of women eligible for self-defence training.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, the first line contains an integer N, the number of women. The second line contains N space‑separated integers A1 … AN, the ages of the women.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing the number of women whose age is between 10 and 60 inclusive.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Test case 1: ages 15 and 23 are within [10,60] → 2.\nTest case 2: ages 15 and 16 are within the range → 2.\nTest case 3: only age 35 is within the range → 1.
Ages 10, 60 and 30 satisfy 10 ≤ age ≤ 60, so the answer is 3.
First test case: no age lies in the interval → 0.\nSecond test case: all four ages are between 10 and 60 inclusive → 4.

solution.approach: For each test case iterate over the N ages and increase a counter when the age satisfies 10 <= age <= 60. After processing all ages output the counter. This runs in O(N) per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N) – each age is examined once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few integer variables are used.
